data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T223610-6b646bdc/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T223610-6b646bdc/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T223610-6b646bdc/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T223610-6b646bdc/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T223610-6b646bdc/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-054b2449">
    <title>feat(metrics): extract per-attempt rows to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Performance analysis across model / prompt / harness / spec
  dimensions today requires fan-out reads of every bead's
  events.jsonl. There is no global per-attempt table with
  denormalized fields ready for query. After sister ADR formalizes
  the storage architecture, this bead implements the cold-tier
  metrics extraction.

  Without this, optimization is blind: we cannot answer "which
  model has the best cost-per-success for our drain" or "which
  prompt template fails most on which spec class."

CURRENT STATE
  - .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl contains lifecycle
    events: routing, cost, review-pass, review-block,
    closed-merged, etc. These rows have the raw signals
    (model, harness, cost_usd, tokens, verdict) needed for
    aggregate analysis.
  - There is no global attempts.jsonl file aggregating across
    beads.
  - Sister beads B29 (retention) cannot safely prune local
    evidence until metrics have been captured.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Define schema for .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl (one row per
     bead-attempt; JSONL, append-only):
        - schema_version: 1 (integer; for future migration)
        - attempt_id: string (current "run-id" until rename
          bead lands, then attempt-id)
        - bead_id: string
        - session_id: string (agent harness session)
        - ts_start, ts_end: RFC3339 timestamps
        - model: string
        - harness: string
        - profile: string (optional)
        - provider: string
        - prompt_template_id: string (optional)
        - prompt_template_hash: string (hash of the rendered
          prompt; enables grouping by prompt variant)
        - spec_id: string (FEAT-N, SD-N, TD-N, ADR-N if known)
        - outcome: string (success | execution_failed |
          review_block | review_fixable_gap | etc.; see TD-031 §5)
        - exit_code: integer
        - cost_usd: float
        - duration_ms: integer
        - input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens: integer
        - review_verdict: string (APPROVE | BLOCK | etc.;
          optional)
        - review_finding_count_by_severity: map of
          severity -> count (optional)
        - ladder_min_power_initial, ladder_min_power_final:
          integer (per ADR-024 ladder semantics)
        - ladder_steps_taken: integer

  2. Forward-going hook:
     - On attempt finalization in
       cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or sibling),
       extract the fields from result.json + manifest.json + the
       attempt's events and append a row to
       .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
     - Use O_APPEND for atomic single-writer appends.
     - On multi-drain machines (future federation): partition
       to .ddx/metrics/<machine-id>/attempts.jsonl (sister bead
       deferred until federation arrives).

  3. One-shot backfill:
     - cli/cmd/work_metrics_backfill.go (new file) implements
       ddx work metrics backfill.
     - For each closed bead, read its events.jsonl (in
       .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl or inline in
       beads.jsonl) and emit one row per attempt found.
     - DO NOT read .ddx/executions/<dir>/ for the backfill;
       events.jsonl is sufficient.
     - Idempotent: skip rows already in attempts.jsonl
       (dedupe by attempt_id).

  4. Tests:
     - TestMetrics_AppendRowOnFinalization: assert a row is
       added per attempt with all expected fields populated.
     - TestMetrics_BackfillFromEvents: assert backfill emits
       correct rows from existing events.
     - TestMetrics_BackfillIdempotent: assert re-running
       backfill does not duplicate rows.
     - TestMetrics_SchemaVersion: assert schema_version field
       is present and equals 1.

NON-SCOPE
  - The analyze CLI (sister bead).
  - Multi-machine partitioning (deferred until federation).
  - Adding agent-log-derived fields (tool_call_count, etc.) —
    those can be added later via schema_version bump.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR): formalize 3-tier evidence storage"
    — this bead implements the cold-tier metrics layer the ADR
    documents.
  - Sister bead B29 (RetainDays prune) DEPENDS on this bead
    landing first; pruning local evidence is safe only after
    metrics are extracted.
  - Sister bead "feat(work): ddx work analyze CLI" reads
    .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl is a JSONL file, one row per bead-attempt; schema includes the fields listed in PROPOSED FIX item 1.
2. Each row has schema_version=1.
3. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or equivalent) appends a row on attempt finalization using O_APPEND.
4. ddx work metrics backfill (or equivalent CLI) reads existing events.jsonl files and produces .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl from existing data, idempotent.
5. TestMetrics_AppendRowOnFinalization, TestMetrics_BackfillFromEvents, TestMetrics_BackfillIdempotent, TestMetrics_SchemaVersion all pass.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:metrics, kind:feature, spec:ADR</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="c3f17803315c236e6f5180cd2b3ec18ba7355415" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T223610-6b646bdc"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 4 required tests pass. The implementation is complete:

**AC verification:**

1. ✅ `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` — JSONL format, one row per attempt, schema includes all fields from the bead spec (`schema_version`, `attempt_id`, `bead_id`, `session_id`, `ts_start`, `ts_end`, `model`, `harness`, `profile`, `provider`, `prompt_template_id`, `prompt_template_hash`, `spec_id`, `outcome`, `exit_code`, `cost_usd`, `duration_ms`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `total_tokens`, `review_verdict`, `review_finding_count_by_severity`, `ladder_min_power_initial`, `ladder_min_power_final`, `ladder_steps_taken`)

2. ✅ `schema_version=1` on every row — verified by `TestMetrics_SchemaVersion`

3. ✅ `execute_bead_loop.go` appends a row on attempt finalization at 4 finalization points (resource exhausted, routing infrastructure, per-bead budget, main finalization) using `appendAttemptMetrics()` → `AppendRow()` → `appendJSONLRecord()` with `O_APPEND`

4. ✅ `ddx work metrics backfill` reads `events.jsonl` files and produces `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`, idempotent by skipping known `attempt_id`s

5. ✅ All 4 tests pass: `TestMetrics_AppendRowOnFinalization`, `TestMetrics_BackfillFromEvents`, `TestMetrics_BackfillIdempotent`, `TestMetrics_SchemaVersion`

6. ✅ `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` — green

7. ✅ `lefthook run pre-commit` — passes
